title: Pressure, Temperature, and Flow Calculator Tool (Frontend)

requirements.summary: Build a single-page calculator UI presenting Pressure, Temperature, and Flow sections with immediate on-page results in English.
acceptanceCriteria: The home screen clearly presents three calculator sections (or tabs): Pressure, Temperature, Flow.
Each section contains labeled input fields and a prominent calculated result area.
All user-facing labels, buttons, and messages are in English.

file_operations.description: Create the main calculator screen UI with three sections (prefer tabs) and wire in the Pressure, Temperature, and Flow calculator components. Use shadcn-ui components (e.g., Tabs, Card, Button) to structure the layout and sections. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create the Pressure calculator section component with labeled inputs and an always-visible result area that updates immediately as the user types. Use shadcn-ui form primitives (e.g., Input, Label, Select) for consistent UI. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create the Temperature conversion section component with labeled inputs, unit selection, and an immediate conversion result area. Use shadcn-ui components for inputs/selects and layout. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create the Flow rate calculator section component with labeled inputs, unit selection, and an immediate result area. Use shadcn-ui components for inputs/selects and layout. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Ensure the document title and language metadata align with the calculator app and English-only UI (keep language set to 'en').

requirements.summary: Implement Pressure, Temperature, and Flow calculations with unit support and show readable formulas in each section.
acceptanceCriteria: Pressure calculator computes correctly for multiple unit combinations (including proper cm²↔m² and N↔kN conversions).
Temperature converter correctly converts between all pairs of (°C, °F, K) and prevents invalid Kelvin (< 0 K).
Flow calculator computes correctly for multiple unit combinations (including L↔m³ and min↔s conversions).
Each calculator section displays the formula being used in readable form.

file_operations.description: Implement pure TypeScript calculation utilities for: Pressure (P = F/A with N/kN and m²/cm² conversions, outputs in Pa and kPa), Temperature conversions between °C/°F/K with Kelvin lower-bound checks, and Flow (Q = V/t with m³/L and s/min conversions, outputs in m³/s and L/s). Keep functions side-effect free for easy UI integration.

Wire the Pressure UI inputs to the calculation utilities, including unit selectors (Force: N, kN; Area: m², cm²) and show outputs in Pa and kPa. Display the formula text 'P = F / A' prominently in the section. Use shadcn-ui Select/Input components for unit selection and inputs. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Wire Temperature unit selectors and numeric input to conversion utilities; support conversions between all pairs of °C/°F/K and show a clear formula/help text for the selected conversion (e.g., °F = °C × 9/5 + 32). Use shadcn-ui Select/Input components. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Wire Flow inputs to utilities with unit selectors (Volume: m³, L; Time: s, min) and show outputs in m³/s and L/s. Display formula text 'Q = V / t' prominently. Use shadcn-ui Select/Input components. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Add robust input validation and inline error states so invalid inputs never produce misleading results or crashes.
acceptanceCriteria: Invalid inputs show an English inline error message near the relevant field(s).
When inputs are invalid, the result area does not show misleading numbers (e.g., shows “—” or “Fix inputs to calculate”).
The app remains responsive and does not throw runtime errors during typing.

file_operations.description: Create shared validation helpers for numeric parsing and field rules (required fields, numeric checks, disallow zero/negative area and time, and Kelvin lower-bound validation). Return field-level error messages suitable for inline display.

Add field-level validation for Force and Area (required, numeric; Area must be > 0). Show inline English errors near fields and ensure the result area shows a non-numeric placeholder message (e.g., 'Fix inputs to calculate') when invalid. Use shadcn-ui components (e.g., Alert or muted text styles) for error presentation. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add validation for temperature input (required, numeric) and prevent invalid Kelvin results (< 0 K) by showing an inline English error and suppressing misleading results when invalid. Use shadcn-ui components for error styling. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add validation for Volume and Time (required, numeric; Time must be > 0). Show inline English errors near fields and suppress results when invalid. Use shadcn-ui components for error styling. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Apply an industrial instrument-panel visual theme with Tailwind: dark/gray surfaces, high-contrast typography, and a consistent non-blue/non-purple accent color; responsive on mobile and desktop.
acceptanceCriteria: The UI has a consistent color palette, spacing, and typography across all calculator sections.
Primary interactive elements (e.g., reset buttons, active tab) use one consistent accent color that is not blue or purple.
Layout works well on mobile and desktop widths.

file_operations.description: Define the global Tailwind base/styles for an industrial instrument-panel look: dark/gray surfaces, high-contrast text, and CSS variables for the theme tokens (including a single non-blue/non-purple accent used for primary/active states). Apply sensible defaults for typography and spacing using Tailwind layers.
Adjust Tailwind theme extensions (if needed) to support the instrument-panel styling and ensure tokens (primary/accent/background/foreground) map cleanly to the CSS variables used in the app theme.

Apply responsive layout and consistent styling across sections (e.g., container widths, spacing, cards/panels, and a consistent non-blue/non-purple accent for active tab and primary actions). Use shadcn-ui layout components (e.g., Card, Tabs, Button) to maintain consistency. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
